test_objectives:
To ensure that the VWO login dashboard functionality meets all security, usability, and performance standards outlined in the Product Requirements Document, 
      including features such as authentication, login process, user input validation, password management, interface design, accessibility features, branding, 
      technical requirements, security specifications, compliance standards, performance metrics, security metrics, and business metrics.
scope:
The test scope includes all functional requirements outlined in the PRD, including authentication system, login process, user input validation, 
      password management, interface design, accessibility features, branding, security specifications, compliance standards, 
      and performance metrics. The scope also includes all non-functional requirements, including security, usability, and performance considerations. 
      However, this scope does not include testing of VWO's core functionality or any third-party integrations outside of the login dashboard.

detailed_test_cases.title: Authentication System
detailed_test_cases.preconditions: The user has a valid VWO account
steps: Step 1: The user navigates to the VWO login page and enters their username and password
Step 2: The user clicks the login button
Step 3: The system authenticates the user credentials and logs them in
detailed_test_cases.expected_result: The user is successfully logged in and redirected to the VWO dashboard

detailed_test_cases.title: Login Process
detailed_test_cases.preconditions: The user has a valid VWO account
steps: Step 1: The user navigates to the VWO login page and clicks the forgot password link
Step 2: The system redirects the user to the forgot password page and prompts them to enter their email address
Step 3: The user enters their email address and clicks submit
Step 4: The system sends a password reset email to the user's email address
detailed_test_cases.expected_result: The user receives a password reset email and is able to reset their password

detailed_test_cases.title: User Input Validation
detailed_test_cases.preconditions: The user has a valid VWO account
steps: Step 1: The user attempts to login with an invalid username
Step 2: The system displays an error message indicating that the username is invalid
Step 3: The user attempts to login with an invalid password
Step 4: The system displays an error message indicating that the password is invalid
detailed_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error message indicating that the username or password is invalid

detailed_test_cases.title: Password Management
detailed_test_cases.preconditions: The user has a valid VWO account
steps: Step 1: The user attempts to change their password
Step 2: The system requires the user to enter their current password and a new password
Step 3: The system verifies the user's current password and updates their password if valid
detailed_test_cases.expected_result: The user's password is successfully updated

detailed_test_cases.title: Interface Design
detailed_test_cases.preconditions: The user has a valid VWO account
steps: Step 1: The user navigates to the VWO login page
Step 2: The system displays a clear and concise login form with input fields for username and password
Step 3: The user submits the login form and is redirected to the VWO dashboard
detailed_test_cases.expected_result: The user is able to successfully login and access the VWO dashboard

detailed_test_cases.title: Accessibility Features
detailed_test_cases.preconditions: The user has a valid VWO account
steps: Step 1: The user navigates to the VWO login page using assistive technology
Step 2: The system displays the login form in a clear and navigable format
Step 3: The user submits the login form and is redirected to the VWO dashboard
detailed_test_cases.expected_result: The user is able to successfully login and access the VWO dashboard using assistive technology

non_functional_scenarios: The system responds within a time interval of 3 seconds or less for a user with a normal connection speed
The system maintains its performance when a large number of concurrent users attempt to login
The system protects user data from unauthorized access and meets relevant security standards
risk_analysis:
The major risks associated with this feature are security risks, such as unsecured password storage or login attempts from unauthorized IP addresses. 
      Additionally, performance risks, such as high latency or slow database queries, could impact the user experience. 
      Compliance risks, such as failure to meet accessibility standards, may also result in fines and reputational damage.
test_data_requirements: Valid user credentials and testing data for performance and security testing
entry_criteria: Valid user credentials and testing data for performance and security testing
